commands.documentation: Replace the current node frame with the target node. Use for lateral or cycling navigation where you do not want to return to the calling node. Contrast with <<jump>> which pushes and returns.
Emit an explicit player speech line to the dialogue history. Useful with #silent on the containing choice to replace the auto-echoed choice label with natural speech.
Emit an explicit NPC speech line to the dialogue history. Equivalent to a plain dialogue line, but useful for clarity when mixing player and NPC lines inside a choice body.
Add an item to the player's inventory.
Remove an item from the player's inventory.
Apply an effect to the player.
Remove an effect from the player.
Make the NPC follow the player.
Make the NPC stop following the player.
Set the NPC's attitude (e.g. NPCATT_FOLLOW, NPCATT_KILL).
Store a value in the player's variable bag. Accepts string, number, or bool.
Delete a variable from the player's variable bag.
Add an amount to a numeric variable in the player's variable bag.
Store a value in the NPC's variable bag. Accepts string, number, or bool.
Delete a variable from the NPC's variable bag.
Add an amount to a numeric variable in the NPC's variable bag.
Legacy: set a named variable on the player using the old talk_var naming scheme (name, type, context, value).
Legacy: set a named variable on the NPC using the old talk_var naming scheme (name, type, context, value).
Legacy: remove a named variable from the player using the old talk_var naming scheme (name, type, context).
Legacy: remove a named variable from the NPC using the old talk_var naming scheme (name, type, context).
Legacy: add an amount to a numeric variable on the player using the old talk_var naming scheme (name, type, context, amount).
Legacy: add an amount to a numeric variable on the NPC using the old talk_var naming scheme (name, type, context, amount).
Call any Lua global by dotted path (e.g. "my_mod.apply_bonus"). Args after the path are forwarded to the Lua function. Return "stop" or false from Lua to end the conversation.
No-op placeholder.
Instant morale boost from pleasant conversation.
Morale boost via a 10-minute socialize activity. Ends the conversation.
Opens the player character display screen.
Ends the conversation. Equivalent to <<stop>>.
Assigns the NPC's currently selected mission to the player.
Creates a new mission of the given type and assigns it to the player.

parameters.name: mission_id
parameters.documentation: The mission type ID to assign.

commands.documentation: Marks the selected mission as complete, improves NPC opinion.
Marks the selected mission as failed, decreases NPC opinion.
Removes the selected mission from the NPC's assigned list.
Opens the trade screen to deliver the mission reward.
NPC leads the player to a safe location via a temporary mission.
NPC gives the player a random item from their inventory.
NPC drops their primary weapon.
Player holsters their weapon.
Player drops all weapons.
Player drops all items flagged as stolen.
Removes the stolen-goods penalty from the player.
Opens the trade screen with this NPC.
Delivers 10 logs to the ranch depot.
Delivers 100 logs to the ranch depot.
Spawns a horse for the player.
Spawns a cow for the player.
Spawns a chicken for the player.
NPC heals the player's injuries. Assigns a 30-minute wait. Ends the conversation.
NPC heals the player and all followers. Assigns a 60-minute wait. Ends the conversation.
Opens the hairstyle selection menu.
Opens the facial hair selection menu.
Gives the player a haircut. Assigns a 20-minute wait. Ends the conversation.
Gives the player a shave. Assigns a 5-minute wait. Ends the conversation.
Opens the bionic installation screen.
Opens the bionic removal screen.
Starts a training session for a skill, martial art, or spell. Ends the conversation.
NPC joins the player's follower list.
NPC begins following the player without formally joining.
NPC stops following the player.
NPC declines to follow (sets cooldown flag).
NPC declines to lead (sets cooldown flag).
NPC declines to give equipment (sets cooldown flag).
NPC declines to train (sets cooldown flag).
NPC declines to share personal info (sets cooldown flag).
Opens a menu to copy rules from another follower.
Opens the NPC's pickup rules editor.
Clears the NPC's rule overrides.
Assigns the NPC to sort loot.
Assigns the NPC to construction work.
Assigns the NPC to mining.
NPC reads a nearby book.
Assigns the NPC to chopping planks.
Assigns the NPC to deconstruct a vehicle.
Assigns the NPC to repair a vehicle.
Assigns the NPC to chop trees.
Assigns the NPC to fish.
Assigns the NPC to farm.
Assigns the NPC to butcher.
NPC stops their current activity and reverts to default behavior.
Sets the NPC on guard duty at their current location.
Removes the NPC's guard duty.
Opens a menu for the NPC to select a destination.
NPC searches for a nearby creature to mount.
NPC dismounts from their current mount.
NPC becomes hostile and attacks the player.
NPC flees from the player.
NPC leaves and becomes independent.
NPC insults the player and starts combat.
NPC begins a mugging attempt.
NPC notices the player is leaving and becomes impatient.
NPC becomes neutral toward the player.
Wakes the NPC from sleep.
Kills the NPC.
NPC becomes friendly and less aggressive.
Player takes direct control of the NPC. Ends the conversation.
Changes the NPC's faction. Does not alter class or stats.
Changes the NPC's class archetype. Does not re-initialize stats or inventory; use <<npc_randomize>> after this to apply the new class's defaults.
Re-rolls the NPC's stats and inventory based on their current class.
Teaches the player a crafting recipe by ID. Displays a confirmation popup.
Sets a reason string that can be tested with has_reason(). Cleared at the start of each conversation.
Charges the player via the NPC trade ledger, then spawns friendly pet monsters near the player. count, pacified, and name are optional (defaults: 1, false, "").

Block that executes its body only the first time it is reached (per player save, globally persistent). Use <<once if expr>> to also require a condition — the condition is checked first and the 'once' is only consumed if it is true. Close with <<endonce>>. Supports an optional <<else>> branch.
Closes a <<once>> block.

Generates one choice per matching item in the player's inventory. The single -> inside uses <topic_item> as a placeholder for the item name. Optional: add #include_containers to search inside containers, or append 'if expr' to add a group-level condition. Close with <<endrepeat>>.
Like <<repeat_for_item>> but matches items by item category ID. Optional: #include_containers, 'if expr'. Close with <<endrepeat>>.
Like <<repeat_for_item>> but searches the NPC's inventory instead of the player's.
Like <<repeat_for_category>> but searches the NPC's inventory.
Closes a <<repeat_for_*>> block.
Player sells all charges of the current item (set by <<repeat_for_item/category>>) to the NPC. The NPC pays the player in faction currency.
NPC sells all charges of the current item to the player. The player pays in faction currency.
Player gives all charges of the current item to the NPC with no payment.
NPC gives all charges of the current item to the player with no payment.

functions.documentation: Returns true if the player has the given mutation/trait.
Returns true if the player has the given effect active.
Returns true if the player has the given bionic installed.
Returns true if the player has at least one of the given item.
Returns true if the player has at least count of the given item.
Returns true if the player's skill level is >= the given level.
Returns the player's current Strength value.
Returns the player's current Dexterity value.
Returns the player's current Intelligence value.
Returns the player's current Perception value.
Returns the player's name.
Returns true if the NPC has the given mutation/trait.
Returns true if the NPC is currently following the player.
Returns true if the NPC has a friendly/allied attitude.
Returns true if the NPC has a hostile attitude.
Returns the NPC's name.
Returns the NPC's trust value toward the player.
Returns a player variable as a string. Returns empty string if not set.
Returns a player variable as a number. Returns 0 if not set.
Returns an NPC variable as a string. Returns empty string if not set.
Returns an NPC variable as a number. Returns 0 if not set.
Returns the NPC's current Strength value.
Returns the NPC's current Dexterity value.
Returns the NPC's current Intelligence value.
Returns the NPC's current Perception value.
Returns true if the NPC has the given effect active.
Returns true if the NPC has the given bionic installed.
Returns true if the NPC's skill level is >= the given level.
Returns the NPC's current level in the given skill.

Call any Lua global by dotted path (e.g. "my_mod.check_rep"). The first argument is the function path string; additional args of any type are forwarded to Lua. Returns the Lua function's return value as a bool, number, or string.
Returns true if the player is male.
Returns true if the player is female.
Returns true if the NPC is male.
Returns true if the NPC is female.
Returns true if the player is wearing the given item.
Returns true if the NPC is wearing the given item.
Returns true if the player is armed (wielding something).
Returns true if the NPC is armed (wielding something).
Returns true if the NPC has at least one of the given item.
Returns true if the NPC has at least count of the given item.
Returns true if the player has any trait with the given flag (e.g. CANNIBAL, SAPIOVORE).
Returns true if the NPC has any trait with the given flag.
Returns the player's hunger level as a number (higher = hungrier).
Returns the player's thirst level as a number (higher = thirstier).
Returns the player's fatigue level as a number (higher = more tired).
Returns the NPC's hunger level as a number.
Returns the NPC's thirst level as a number.
Returns the NPC's fatigue level as a number.
Returns the player's electronic cash (e-cash) balance.
Returns how much this NPC owes the player.
Returns true if the NPC is not busy.
Returns true if the NPC is mounted on a creature.
Returns true if the NPC has an active player_activity.
Returns true if the NPC belongs to the given class (e.g. NC_SOLDIER).
Returns true if the NPC is a player ally.
Returns true if the NPC is hostile to the player.
Returns true if this NPC has no missions assigned to the player.
Returns true if this NPC has exactly one mission assigned to the player.
Returns true if this NPC has more than one mission assigned.
Returns true if this NPC has no missions available to offer.
Returns true if this NPC has exactly one mission available.
Returns true if this NPC has more than one mission available.
Returns true if the currently selected mission has been completed.
Returns true if the currently selected mission is not yet complete.
Returns the current season as a string: "spring", "summer", "autumn", or "winter".
Returns true if the current season matches the given string ("spring", "summer", "autumn", "winter").
Returns the number of days elapsed since the start of the cataclysm.
Returns true if it is currently daytime (sun above horizon).
Returns true if the player is currently driving a moving vehicle.
Returns true if the NPC is currently driving a moving vehicle.
Returns true if the NPC's ally rule is active for the given key (e.g. "use_guns", "allow_sleep"). Considers overrides.
Returns true if an override is set for the given ally rule key (e.g. "use_guns").
Returns true if the NPC's aim rule matches the given value (e.g. "AIM_PRECISE", "AIM_SPRAY").
Returns true if the NPC's engagement rule matches the given value (e.g. "ENGAGE_ALL", "ENGAGE_CLOSE").
Returns true if the NPC's CBM power reserve rule matches the given value (e.g. "CBM_RESERVE_ALL", "CBM_RESERVE_HALF").
Returns true if the NPC's CBM recharge rule matches the given value (e.g. "CBM_RECHARGE_ALWAYS", "CBM_RECHARGE_NEVER").
Returns true if the NPC is currently outdoors (not under a roof).
Returns true if the NPC is in a safe overmap tile.
Returns true if the player is on the given overmap terrain type (e.g. "shelter").
Returns true if the NPC is on the given overmap terrain type.
Returns true if any NPC within 48 tiles has the given companion mission role ID.
Returns true if the player has at least the given number of NPC allies.
Returns true if the NPC has at least one skill they can teach the player.
Returns true if the NPC has at least one martial art style they can teach the player.
Returns true if the player is carrying any item that belongs to this NPC.
Returns true if the player has an active mission of the given mission type ID.
Returns true if the player knows the given recipe.
Returns true if this conversation is happening over a radio connection.
Returns true if a reason string has been set via <<set_reason>> during this conversation.
